# systemd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `systemd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `systemd.user.automounts` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd automount unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user automount units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.automount(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.automounts.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.automounts.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the user systemd service manager. |
| `systemd.user.mounts` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd mount unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user mount units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.mount(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.mounts.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.mounts.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.packages` | `list of package` | Packages providing systemd user units. This is the Home Manager equivalent of NixOS’s `systemd.packages` option. Files in {file}`«pkg»/share/systemd/user` will be included in the user’s {file}`$XDG_DATA_HOME/systemd/user` directory. |
| `systemd.user.paths` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd path unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user path units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.path(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.paths.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.paths.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.services` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd service unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user service units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.service(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Service.Environment` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | Environment variables available to executed processes. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Service.ExecStart` | `package or string or list of (package or string)` | Command that is executed when this service is started. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Unit.X-Reload-Triggers` | `list of (package or string)` | List of free form strings that can be used to trigger a service reload during Home Manager activation. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Unit.X-Restart-Triggers` | `list of (package or string)` | List of free form strings that can be used to trigger a service restart during Home Manager activation. |
| `systemd.user.services.<name>.Unit.X-SwitchMethod` | `one of <null>, "reload", "restart", "stop-start", "keep-old"` | The preferred method to use when switching from an old to a new version of this service. |
| `systemd.user.servicesStartTimeoutMs` | `unsigned integer, meaning >=0` | How long to wait for started services to fail until their start is considered successful. The value 0 indicates no timeout. |
| `systemd.user.sessionVariables` | `attribute set of (signed integer or string)` | Environment variables that will be set for the user session. The variable values must be as described in {manpage}`environment.d(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string) or a list of them for duplicate keys)` | Extra config options for user session service manager. See {manpage}`systemd-user.conf(5)` for available options. |
| `systemd.user.settings.Manager.DefaultEnvironment` | `attribute set of (null or string or absolute path or package)` | Configures environment variables passed to all executed processes. |
| `systemd.user.settings.Manager.ManagerEnvironment` | `attribute set of (null or string or absolute path or package)` | Sets environment variables just for the manager process itself. |
| `systemd.user.slices` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd slice unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user slice units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.slice(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.slices.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.slices.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.sockets` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd socket unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user socket units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.socket(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.sockets.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.sockets.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.startServices` | `boolean or one of "suggest", "sd-switch"` | Whether new or changed services that are wanted by active targets should be started. Additionally, stop obsolete services from the previous generation. The alternatives are `suggest` (or `false`) : Use a very simple shell script to print suggested {command}`systemctl` commands to run. You will have to manually run those commands after the switch. `sd-switch` (or `true`) : Use sd-switch, a tool that determines the necessary changes and automatically apply them. |
| `systemd.user.systemctlPath` | `string` | Absolute path to the {command}`systemctl` tool. This option may need to be set if running Home Manager on a non-NixOS distribution. |
| `systemd.user.targets` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd target unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user target units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.target(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.targets.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.targets.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.timers` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd timer unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user timer units. Attributes are merged recursively. Note that the attributes follow the capitalization and naming used by systemd. More details can be found in {manpage}`systemd.timer(5)`. |
| `systemd.user.timers.<name>.Unit.Description` | `null or string` | A short human-readable label of the unit. |
| `systemd.user.timers.<name>.Unit.Documentation` | `(list of string) or string convertible to it` | List of URIs referencing documentation for the unit. |
| `systemd.user.tmpfiles.rules` | `list of string` | Rules for creating and cleaning up temporary files automatically. See {manpage}`tmpfiles.d(5)` for the exact format. |
